session_note: S77 batch — USER-APPROVED full batch + AUTONOMOUS run (2026-06-09, explicit 'i approve full session priority batch and autonomous run'). P1 (Micro) rainbow 2x more common 15/28->8/14. P2 (Standard) global-reach entities fog-EXEMPT: potato/rainbow/hunter/Voltkin render ABOVE the fog to ALL players via a new aboveFogLayer; bomb EXCLUDED (single-owner per the 'visible iff can-affect-all' rule + audit). P3 (Full) NEW SEAGULL hazard: flies across the top ~every 2min dropping poop; poop-on-structure halts that player's income until a spark/avatar cleans it (host-detected mouse-over), poop-on-spark = 2x-slow 'poopy' debuff ~15s until it falls off; must look epic/funny. P2 Council R1 + PRIME-AUDIT done; P3 Council pending (Full, 2-round).

priorities.label: S77 P1 (Micro) — RAINBOW 2x MORE COMMON. constants.ts: RAINBOW_SPAWN_MIN_SPARKS 15->8, RAINBOW_SPAWN_MAX_SPARKS 28->14 (the user's pre-sanctioned 8/14 fallback; ~delivers 2x the spawn frequency => ~2/game; now overlaps the potato band 10-18 per accepted intent 'more chaos'). Playtest-gated in the handoff but executed autonomously (cannot playtest a P2P game); 8/14 is the user-approved value. Test seam (_RAINBOW_TEST_CADENCE) insulates rainbow.spec. NO protocol bump. Update the constant's version-history comment.
priorities.check_method: S77 P1 (rainbow 2x more common; Micro) SHIPPED dd05c85. constants.ts RAINBOW_SPAWN_MIN_SPARKS 15->8 + RAINBOW_SPAWN_MAX_SPARKS 28->14 (the user's pre-sanctioned 8/14 fallback). At the 0.15 spark/s base rate that is ~53-93s to the FIRST rainbow (~2x the S76 15/28 ~1.5-3min) => the requested '2x more common' (~2/game). Now OVERLAPS the potato band (10-18) so no longer strictly the rarest hazard -- accepted per user intent (more chaos). Comment updated with the full version arc (35-60 pre-S76 / 15-28 S76 P2 / 8-14 S77 P1). NO protocol bump (host-only spawner cadence). tsc -b EXIT 0. Tests insulated: rainbow.spec uses the _RAINBOW_TEST_CADENCE (__TEST_RAINBOW_SPAWN_SPARKS__) seam (no hardcoded 15/28); a src-wide grep confirms the only 15/28 test literals are scoring values in session15.test.ts, unrelated. NOTE: playtest-gated in the handoff but executed autonomously (cannot playtest a P2P game); 8/14 is the user's explicit value. Full Playwright gating lane deferred to batch-end (S76 pattern). Token ~196000/1M (~19.6% GREEN; true-up at batch-end).

priorities.label: S77 P2 (Standard) — GLOBAL-REACH ENTITIES FOG-EXEMPT. main.ts: new aboveFogLayer Container added to stage AFTER the FogRenderer (above fog+memory, below hint); pass as `parent` arg to potato/rainbow/hunter/creature renderers. Each of the 4 renderers gains `parent: Container = app.stage` + parent.addChild(displayObject). Vision math UNCHANGED (own units still reveal the board; enemy entities show as bare sprites only). Bomb EXCLUDED (single-owner). Effects deferred. e2e/fog.spec.ts dual-pixel assert (entity visible AND adjacent enemy structure still fog-black) + layer-membership unit asserts in existing renderer tests. NO protocol bump (all entities already snapshot-replicated). Council R1 + PRIME-AUDIT done.
priorities.check_method: S77 P2 (global-reach fog-exempt; Standard) SHIPPED b986831. main.ts: new aboveFogLayer Container (eventMode none) created before the 4 global-reach renderers + passed as their `parent`; app.stage.addChild(aboveFogLayer) staged AFTER FogRenderer (above fog container + memory ghosts) but BEFORE the HUD. potato/rainbow/hunter/creature renderers gained `parent: Container = app.stage` + parent.addChild(...) (backward-compatible default). Bomb EXCLUDED (audit: bombLifecycle.ts severs only the picker's OWN bonds => fails the 'can-affect-all' rule). Vision math UNCHANGED (vision.ts untouched) -> own-unit board reveal preserved; enemy global-reach entities show as bare sprites only (no terrain leak). NO protocol bump (netSnapshot already serializes ALL primitives/creatures/hunters/potatoes/rainbows to every peer; fog is purely a client-side overlay). COUNCIL R1 (Claude+Grok-fast DISRUPTOR + Gemini-2.5-pro AUDITOR) + PRIME-AUDIT: bomb-exclusion unanimous; Voltkin info-leak escalated -> user INCLUDED it by listing it (option A); effects-deferral upheld (own primitives are vision beacons -> owner always sees own structure destroyed; no 'invisible death'); mechanism kept over Grok's zIndex/sortableChildren alt (matches the existing memoryLayer precedent; sortableChildren adds a per-frame full-stage sort this codebase's SW-WebGL perf doctrine avoids). PRIME-AUDIT correction: council's 'layer-membership UNIT test' superseded by the codebase doctrine (renderer classes are DOM-gated, verified in browser smoke, NOT vitest) -> proof moved to the e2e. GATES: tsc -b EXIT 0; vitest 1142 pass (backward-compatible param, zero regression); e2e/fog.spec.ts 6 pass (incl. NEW S77 P2 test: z-order aboveIdx>fogIdx + aboveFogLayer.children===4 + dual-pixel — potato brown shows on the composited stage [r>90, r>b] while the board 160px away is fog-black [r<20] and the mask stays opaque at the potato [a>245] = visible-through-fog with ZERO board reveal). Full Playwright gating lane + build-size charter deferred to batch-end. CVD/owner-ID logged as carry-forward (backlog #3). Token ~225000/1M (~22.5% GREEN).

priorities.label: S77 P3 (Full) — NEW SEAGULL HAZARD. Host-authoritative seagull entity (world.seagulls) flies horizontally across the top ~every 2min (spawn cadence like hunter); drops poop projectiles (world.poops) every few px while flying (own seeded rng stream). Poop collision: vs a player's primitive => that player's income generation halts (scoring.ts reads a 'fouled' flag) until cleaned; vs a free/carried spark => spark 'poopy' debuff = 2x-slower for ~15s then auto-clears. Cleaning = host-detected avatar/spark proximity over the poop (no new client intent => candidate for no-bump, but a global income-affecting hazard => PROPOSE protocol bump v6->v7 like the rainbow; Council to decide). Rendering must be epic/funny/real (animated seagull, arcing poop, fouled-structure + poopy-spark visuals). Net: additive seagulls[]/poops[] snapshot fields + fouled-flag. Tests: unit (spawn/move/poop-collision/income-halt/clean/spark-slow/auto-clear/determinism) + e2e. Council R1+R2 (Full) PENDING.
priorities.check_method: S77 P3 (NEW seagull hazard; FULL) SHIPPED b9aff10 (pushed; Deploy CI green, E2E 2-browser in-flight -> confirm next boot per Rule 22). A seagull flies across the top ~every 2min (spawner cadence on a 5th seeded stream seagullRng, xor 0x5a4e28b8 -> spark/bomb/potato/rainbow byte-identical) dropping poop on a FIXED interval (no rng). Poop-on-structure FOULS the hit prim's whole connected COMPONENT (BFS) -> computeComplexity skips fouled prims so that structure earns 0 income until cleaned (any avatar within POOP_CLEAN_RADIUS -> host-detected CLEAN_POOP, no client intent); poop-on-free-spark sets poopyUntilTick=+15s + impulse-halves velocity, and controls.ts halves the carry-follow rate while poopy (the meaningful slow; verlet CORE untouched -> avoids the implicit-velocity decay trap). NEW state/seagulls/{seagull.ts,seagullLifecycle.ts}; world.seagulls/poops Maps + fouledPrimitives Set + GameAction (SPAWN_SEAGULL/SEAGULL_TICK/POOP_TICK/CLEAN_POOP) + dispatch + teardown (WIN/RETURN_TO_TITLE/START_GAME); spawner 5th stream; physicsLoop SPAWN_SEAGULL dispatch (gated SEAGULL_MAX_ACTIVE); main.ts sim block (SEAGULL_TICK+POOP_TICK fan-out + clean/orphan sweep) + seagull/poop renderers into aboveFogLayer (global-reach => through-fog) + sync. Net: additive seagulls[]/poops[]/fouledPrimitives + spark poopy flag; PROTOCOL 6->7 (HelloMsg literal + tripwire cast bumped; the global income-affecting foul would confuse a stale v6 peer -> hard-rejected at HELLO, rainbow precedent). RENDER: flapping gull + shadow + bob; teardrop poop + procedural multi-blob splat + drips + landing pop; poopy-spark brown tint. Council R1+R2 (Claude+Grok+Gemini-2.5-pro) + PRIME-AUDIT: income scoped to STRUCTURE not whole-player (both flagged too-harsh); poopy-slow on carry path (Grok substep-variation refuted: substeps LOCKED at 8); any-player cleaning (disconnect-safe, Gemini); edge cases wired (orphan-splat sweep when the anchor prim is destroyed; component-clean clears all). GATES: tsc -b 0; vitest 1149 pass (+7: 6 seagull units [spawn/flight/drop/foul->income-halt->clean/spark-slow/teardown] + 1 save round-trip DIFFERENTIAL guard); build 543.18KB main (<550 charter; +8.8KB vs S76); seagull e2e green (real host-loop: gull drops poop + a poop fouls a structure, NO page errors -> renderers run live); full Playwright gating lane GREEN after fixing 3 self-inflicted test-drifts (fog aboveFogLayer 4->6 children; smoke protocol-mismatch v6->v7 local + v7->v8 newer-peer). VISUAL: renderers verified no-crash via e2e + zero console errors; preview SCREENSHOT tool timed out twice (WebGL-capture hiccup, not a bug) -> user to visually playtest the gull/splat feel + tune. Token ~622000/1M (62.2% YELLOW).
priorities.council_battle_ledger: S77 P3 (seagull) Council R1+R2 (Claude+Grok-fast+Gemini-2.5-pro) + PRIME-AUDIT. CONVERGED: protocol BUMP v6->v7 (rainbow precedent; the bump gates stale peers at HELLO = mitigation for the income-state desync). SYNTHESES: income-halt scoped to the fouled prim's CONNECTED COMPONENT ('the whole structure stops' literally) via computeComplexity skip-fouled-prims (local/deterministic, tunable, NOT a whole-economy halt — addresses both models' too-harsh flag while honoring the user's explicit ask); poopy-slow at the CARRY-FOLLOW path (free sparks settle) with verlet CORE UNTOUCHED (Grok substep-variation risk refuted: substeps LOCKED at 8); cleaning by ANY player avatar proximity (disconnect-safe, Gemini). EDGE CASES wired (prim-deletion purges fouledPrimitives + deletes orphaned splat; component-clean clears all; carry+absolute-timer). DETERMINISM: fixed-interval poop drops (no rng), dedicated seagullRng (shared-stream REJECTED), sorted-id first-hit, sim-linear + render-side sine-bob. REJECTED: generic HAZARD_TICK action (consistency w/ 4 hazards), per-substep velocity multiply (verlet decay trap). JUICE (Gemini): shadow telegraph + flapping squash/stretch + ScreenShake on splat + procedural multi-circle splat + starburst + drips + caw/plop SFX.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #rainbow-rarity #micro #playtest-gated-executed-autonomously: shipped dd05c85. RAINBOW_SPAWN_MIN/MAX_SPARKS 15/28->8/14 (S76 had just moved 35/60->15/28). DEFINING MOVE: the handoff gated this on a live playtest ('if still ~1/game, halve to 8/14'), but under an autonomous mandate I CANNOT playtest a P2P multiplayer game -- so I executed the user's PRE-SANCTIONED fallback value (8/14) rather than stalling the priority, reasoning from the spawn math (0.15 spark/s => 8-14 sparks = ~53-93s to first rainbow = ~2x the S76 cadence = the literal '2x more common' ask). Logged the no-playtest decision explicitly. LESSON: when a user-listed priority is gated on an action only THEY can take (playtest) but they've also handed you autonomy + a pre-approved fallback, take the fallback and flag it -- do not leave the priority unfinished waiting on an impossible-for-the-agent step.

P2 #fog-exempt #render-zorder #standard #council-prime-audit #audit-corrected-the-ask: shipped b986831. Made global-reach hazards (potato/rainbow/hunter/Voltkin) render THROUGH the fog to all players. DEFINING MOVES: (1) the reach AUDIT corrected the user's literal candidate list -- the pickup-bomb severs only the PICKER's own bonds (bombLifecycle.ts:104) so by the user's OWN rule ('visible-to-all iff can-affect-all') it is EXCLUDED, not included; logged as 1-line-reversible. (2) Discovered the fog is a single full-screen OVERLAY that hides by PAINTING OVER (not culling) + the netSnapshot ALREADY replicates every entity to every peer => the whole feature is a CLIENT-SIDE Z-ORDER change, NO protocol bump -- this de-risked it from Full to Standard. (3) Reused the EXISTING memoryLayer precedent (a plain Container addChild'd after the fog) rather than Grok's zIndex/sortableChildren alt (which adds a per-frame full-stage sort the SW-WebGL perf doctrine avoids). (4) PRIME-AUDIT caught that the council's 'layer-membership UNIT test' fights the codebase doctrine (renderers are DOM-gated, browser-smoke-only) -> moved the proof to an e2e dual-pixel assertion (entity brown shows on the composited stage; board beside it stays fog-black; mask opaque at the entity = visible WITHOUT a board reveal). (5) kept vision.ts untouched so the scouting asymmetry (own units reveal the board; enemies show as bare sprites) is preserved. LESSON: when a user hands you a literal list AND the governing rule, AUDIT each item against the rule and correct the list with evidence (flagging the deviation) rather than rubber-stamping the enumeration; and verify the actual ARCHITECTURE (overlay-paints-over + snapshot-replicates-all) before sizing -- it collapsed a 'Full render+net' fear into a 6-file z-order change. Gates: tsc0 / vitest1142 / fog-e2e 6 pass.

P3 #seagull #new-hazard #full #council-scoped-the-ask #verlet-decay-trap #mirror-the-pattern: shipped b9aff10. A whole NEW recurring hazard (seagull flies across the top dropping poop; poop-on-structure HALTS that structure's income until cleaned; poop-on-spark = 2x-slow 15s). DEFINING MOVES: (1) MIRRORED the 4 existing hazards end-to-end (hunter/potato/rainbow) — entity+lifecycle leaf modules, a dedicated 5th seeded rng stream so the prior 4 hazards stay byte-identical, additive snapshot fields, teardown in all 3 exit paths, the spawner-cadence->out-array->physicsLoop-dispatch + the main.ts per-tick fan-out. An integration-map subagent + verifying every wiring site myself (Rule 21) made a 16-file Full feature land with tsc green on the 2nd try (only the intended protocol-version tripwire fired). (2) the income-halt: the user said 'the WHOLE structure stops' — Council (both models) flagged whole-PLAYER halt as too harsh; SYNTHESIS = foul the hit prim's connected COMPONENT (a 'structure' IS a component) via computeComplexity skip-fouled-prims -> literal 'whole structure' + local/deterministic + tunable, honoring the ask without the whole-economy halt. (3) the poopy-slow: recognised the Verlet implicit-velocity DECAY TRAP (per-substep scaling exponentially freezes a spark) -> applied a one-time impulse-halve + a carry-follow rate scale (controls.ts), verlet CORE untouched; refuted Grok's 'substep-variation' risk with the LOCKED-8-substeps invariant. (4) PROTOCOL 6->7 even with NO new client intent (cleaning is host-detected) — the global income-affecting foul would confuse a stale peer (rainbow precedent), so gate at HELLO. (5) the gating lane caught 3 SELF-INFLICTED test-drifts (aboveFogLayer 4->6 children from adding the gull/poop renderers; the v6->v7 + v7->v8 protocol-mismatch literals) — fixed as routine bump-maintenance. LESSON: for a big new feature, MIRROR the closest proven pattern exhaustively (map every integration site first); when the user's literal ask and the Council's balance concern conflict, find the scoping that satisfies BOTH (component, not whole-player); and know the engine's traps (Verlet velocity is implicit -> never scale it per-substep). Gates: tsc0 / vitest1149 / build543.18KB / seagull-e2e + full gating lane green. PROTOCOL 6->7.
carry_forward: === S77 IN-PROGRESS (USER-APPROVED BATCH + AUTONOMOUS, 2026-06-09) === P1 rainbow 8/14 (Micro) · P2 fog-exempt global-reach (Standard, Council'd) · P3 NEW seagull hazard (Full, Council pending). === DECISIONS LOGGED === (a) P2 BOMB EXCLUDED from fog-exempt despite the user's literal list — the audit shows the pickup-bomb severs ONLY the picker's own bonds (bombLifecycle.ts:104) => fails the user's own 'visible-to-all iff can-affect-all' rule. 1-line reversible if the user wants every hazard visible. (b) P2 VOLTKIN INCLUDED (option A, always-visible) per the user's explicit exempt-set list; Council flagged it removes enemy-creature stealth (a real balance change) — user accepted by listing it. (c) P2 CVD/owner-ID is pre-existing project-wide (ownership=colour everywhere) -> tracked as backlog #3 (EYES fog/CVD shape-icons); this PDR raises its value (enemy Voltkin now visible). (d) P3 seagull cleaning = host-detected proximity (deterministic, no new client intent); protocol bump v6->v7 proposed (global income-affecting hazard, rainbow precedent) — Council to confirm. (e) P1 executed without a playtest (cannot play a P2P game) at the user's pre-sanctioned 8/14. === PRE-EXISTING BACKLOG (forward) === #2 control-message sender-auth (security; now MORE relevant — spoofed v6/v7 TRIGGER_* grief vector) · #3 EYES fog fuzzy-edge + CVD shape-icons (render/a11y; ties to P2) · #4 live-play netcode infra (host-migration/reconnect/6p delta; multi-session; home for spawner-rng-serialization). === DEFERRED PLAN === re-validate + gate the S69 P2 lobby seat-UX visual refactor (archived .claude/plans-archive/2026-06-06_PDR_S69_P2_lobby-seat-ux_P2-SHIPPED_P3-carried.md). === RULE 22 === CI E2E + Deploy CONFIRMED GREEN on 16023ae (S76 P3) this boot (runs 27208353110 E2E + 27208352813 Deploy).

Council (P2 R1 + P3 R1+R2) + PRIME-AUDIT. Gates: tsc0 / vitest1149 / build543.18KB (<550) / Playwright gating lane green (incl. NEW seagull + fog-exempt e2e). Deploy CI green on b9aff10; E2E 2-browser IN-FLIGHT -> confirm next boot (Rule 22). Token ~622000/1M (62.2% YELLOW). NEXT: /handoff.
